---
title: "Grid Congestion in PJM"
subtitle: "A research website on nodal price divergence, congestion signals, renewable stress periods, and transmission investment implications"
---

::: {.hero-kicker}
Research website
:::

This site publishes the PJM-focused output of the `us-grid-congestion-monitor` pipeline. It uses nodal electricity price data to study how renewable buildout interacts with transmission constraints in **PJM** and what those patterns may imply for transmission investment, storage deployment, and grid modernization.

::: {.split-lead}
::: 
The project is organized around four PJM questions:

1. Which PJM regions show persistent price separation?
2. To what extent can observed nodal price spread be interpreted as a congestion signal?
3. Are abnormal spread and negative prices more common during high-renewable or low-net-load intervals?
4. What do these patterns imply for transmission investment, storage deployment, and grid modernization in PJM?

::: {.inline-links}
[Jump to Findings](#findings)
[Jump to Maps](#maps)
[Jump to Methodology](#methodology)
[Jump to Downloads](#data-downloads)
:::
:::
:::

## What this site contains

::: {.section-band}
- A narrative summary of the four core research questions
- Interactive PJM congestion maps
- A node-to-hub spread animation layer
- Methodology notes on harmonization, hub references, and congestion metrics
- Download links for map-ready and report-ready exports
:::

## Current project framing

The published analysis pipeline follows this sequence:

1. Download PJM raw ISO data through `gridstatus`
2. Clean PJM market-specific schema
3. Harmonize PJM nodal observations into the analytical frame
4. Build congestion, spread, renewable, and hub-reference features
5. Generate charts, maps, export packs, and a report

::: {.small-note}
The website assumes the latest site artifacts are generated by the Python pipeline before publication. On GitHub Pages, Quarto renders the site shell while existing map HTML and CSV exports are copied into the published output.
:::

## {#findings .section-anchor}Key Findings

This project is designed around four analytical questions rather than around raw pipeline stages. That matters because the same metric can play different roles depending on whether we are diagnosing a PJM bottleneck, testing a hypothesis about renewable buildout, or framing a transmission implication.

::: {.map-grid}
::: {.finding-card}
### 1. Persistent price separation
Core indicators:

- `price_separation`
- `abs_node_to_hub_spread`
- `persistent_congestion_score`
:::

::: {.finding-card}
### 2. Spread as congestion signal
Core indicators:

- `avg_abs_congestion`
- `congestion_share_of_lmp`
- overlap between large spread and high congestion intervals
:::

::: {.finding-card}
### 3. Renewable and net-load stress
Core indicators:

- `renewable_penetration`
- `net_load_mw`
- `negative_price_flag`
:::

::: {.finding-card}
### 4. Investment implications
Core interpretation:

- transmission upgrade candidates
- storage siting opportunities
- modernization priorities where divergence persists
:::
:::

The site treats **spread as a candidate congestion signal**, not as automatic proof of congestion. A large node-to-hub spread becomes more convincing when it coincides with elevated congestion components, persistence across intervals, and recurring stress conditions in the same location.

## {#maps .section-anchor}Interactive Maps

### PJM congestion map

<iframe class="map-frame" src="maps/pjm_congestion_map.html" title="PJM congestion map"></iframe>

### Node-to-hub spread animation

<iframe class="map-frame" src="maps/spread_animation.html" title="Node-to-hub spread animation"></iframe>

::: {.section-band}
- Use the toggle in the main maps to switch between congestion intensity and node-to-hub spread.
- Bubble size reflects a persistent congestion score rather than a single-interval spike.
- Hover panels show zone, spread, congestion, negative-price frequency, and hub-reference source.
- The animation layer helps distinguish structural bottlenecks from short-lived price events.
:::

## {#methodology .section-anchor}Methodology

### Pipeline design

The analytical pipeline is:

1. `gridstatus` download layer for PJM
2. PJM-specific cleaning
3. Analytical harmonization into the working nodal schema
4. Feature engineering
5. Congestion metrics
6. Map and report export

### Core data fields

The harmonized schema centers on:

- `timestamp`
- `node_id`
- `node_name`
- `lmp`
- `energy`
- `congestion`
- `loss`
- optional `renewable_mw`, `load_mw`, `zone`, `latitude`, `longitude`

### Congestion metrics

::: {.metric-table}
| Metric | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `abs_congestion` | Direct congestion intensity |
| `price_separation` | Market-wide node divergence in a given interval |
| `hub_lmp` | Reference price used for node-to-hub comparisons |
| `node_to_hub_spread` | Local divergence from a configured market reference |
| `persistent_congestion_score` | Composite score for map prominence and screening |
| `negative_price_flag` | Abnormal pricing indicator |
| `renewable_penetration` | Renewable share proxy when load is available |
| `net_load_mw` | Load minus renewable generation |
:::

### Hub reference logic

`node_to_hub_spread` is configuration-driven. The pipeline first checks [config/markets.yml](config/markets.yml) for explicit PJM hub-reference rules. If a configured match is available, it is used as the reference LMP. If not, the code falls back to heuristic hub detection and then, if needed, to market mean.

## {#data-downloads .section-anchor}Data and Downloads

The live ingestion layer is built around `gridstatus`, which provides a unified interface to PJM LMP, load, and interconnection queue data. The website publishes processed analytical layers rather than full raw ISO dumps.

- [Market summary](data/exports/market_summary.csv)
- [Node summary](data/exports/node_summary.csv)
- [Stress events](data/exports/stress_events.csv)
- [Renewable impact](data/exports/renewable_impact.csv)
- [Harmonized feature layer](data/exports/harmonized_features.csv)
- [PJM map layer CSV](data/exports/pjm_map_layer.csv)
- [PJM animation layer CSV](data/exports/pjm_animation_layer.csv)
- [Latest markdown report](reports/final_report.md)
